feedback.text: As a citizen of the European Economic Area, I want to see a greater protection of consumers right of ownership. Especially when it comes to expanding digital products; such as films, music and video games. To ensure they can not be taken from you by barring access to purchased digital goods, or revoking/removing the product from your digital library. Especially games nowadays have built in anti-consumer practices that allows the developer/publisher to "shut-down" the game. We need to ensure that after a developer/publisher no longer wishes or deems it profitable - that the product can still be used, or in case of video games, played. Earlier games did this by simply providing that game without any remote means of disabling the product, or in case of multiplayer which required a server, provide the server executable and/or a means of self-hosting. This have been done ion the past and can be done again!
